title: Phase 3: Workflow Primitives
description: Build the structured workflow primitives that turn ethos identities and roles into runtime contracts. Mission contracts, write-set admission, frozen evaluators, bounded rounds, independent verification, structured handoff artifacts, append-only mission log. Discipline as runtime contracts, not text in personality files. Source material: agents-architecture.tex and instructions-memory-architecture.tex. See docs/ETHOS-ROADMAP.md Phase 3.

title: Feature-dev ethos integration — review agents as identities
description: Feature-dev's code-reviewer and silent-failure-hunter become ethos identities with personas. Roles provide tool restrictions and anti-responsibilities. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.1.

title: Structured handoff result artifacts (Phase 3.6)
description: Every worker emits typed result: verdict (pass/fail/escalate), confidence, files_changed, evidence (test runs), open_questions, optional prose. Schema validation on ethos mission close — refuse to close without valid result. Leader synthesizes structured fields, not prose. Depends on 3.1. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.6.

title: Append-only mission event log (Phase 3.7)
description: JSON-lines event log per mission at ~/.punt-labs/ethos/missions/<id>.jsonl. Events: create, update, close (Phase 3.1), reflect (Phase 3.4), verify (Phase 3.5), result (Phase 3.6). Append-only, no edit/delete. Source of truth — derived summaries (memory, beads) are convenience layers. Phase 3.7 adds the public READER API (Store.LoadEvents) plus ethos mission log <id> CLI and MCP method. Depends on 3.1. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.7 and DES-037.

title: Define 4 specialized review identities in team registry
description: code-reviewer, security-reviewer, test-reviewer, style-reviewer. Each with persona, talents, role, and anti-responsibilities. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.2.

title: Memory consolidation: reflect talent + session_context reminder
description: Add reflect talent teaching structured reflection pattern. Provide extension session_context that reminds agents to capture learnings in quarry. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.3.

title: Formalize evaluation discipline in workflow
description: Frozen evaluators per review cycle, mixed verification tracks (deterministic + tool + model + human), bounded rounds with mandatory reflection gates, concrete escalation signals (plateau, divergence, budget). See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.5.

title: Mission contract: typed delegation artifact (Phase 3.1)
description: Schema and runtime for the mission contract: inputs, outputs, write_set, tools, success_criteria, evaluator, budget. New internal/mission/ package. CLI: ethos mission create/show/close. MCP: mission tool. Storage: ~/.punt-labs/ethos/missions/<id>.yaml. Foundation for all other Phase 3 primitives. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.1.

title: Write-set admission control (Phase 3.2)
description: Write-set tracking and conflict detection on mission create. Active mission write sets recorded in session-scoped registry. New mission with overlapping write set must wait or isolate in worktree. Depends on 3.1. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.2.

title: Frozen evaluator with content hash pinning (Phase 3.3)
description: Pin evaluator at mission launch via content hash of personality + writing_style + talents + success_criteria. SubagentStart hook validates hash before injecting persona. Drift detected and surfaced — no silent goalpost moving. Depends on 3.1. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.3.

title: Bounded rounds with mandatory reflection (Phase 3.4)
description: Mission contracts declare round budget. After each round, ethos forces structured reflection step before next round can start. Reflection schema: converging, signals (plateau/divergence/budget), recommendation (pivot/escalate/continue/stop), reason. Block on next round if reflection missing. Depends on 3.1. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.4.

title: Independent verification — verifier isolation (Phase 3.5)
description: Verifier subagent receives only mission contract and deltas — never implementer's scratch state. Spawned in separate subagent context with restricted file allowlist from mission write set. Verifier handle must differ from worker handle; role overlap check on mission create. SubagentStart hook strips parent transcript. Depends on 3.1. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.5.

title: Remove -dev command files from prod plugin releases
description: The -dev.md command files ship in prod because ethos has no release-plugin.sh/restore-dev-plugin.sh scripts. Per punt-kit/standards/plugins.md §Release flow: at release time, swap plugin.json to prod name and remove -dev command files from the tagged commit. Then restore dev state on main. Fix: create scripts/release-plugin.sh and scripts/restore-dev-plugin.sh, integrate with punt release.
close_reason: Fixed by PR #39 — release scripts strip -dev files from tags

title: fix: Linux hook stdin hang — shell reads stdin, not Go (DES-029)
description: Ethos hooks hung silently on Linux. Claude Code spawns hooks via /bin/sh -c, making /dev/stdin inaccessible to the Go binary. All 6 hook scripts now use bash read -t 1 + printf to normalize stdin over a fresh pipe. Includes Go-level readWithTimeout defense-in-depth, subprocess integration tests, fake Claude spawn regression test (Node.js), Linux proc tests, and DES-029/DES-030 ADRs.

title: No subcommand-level --help flag parsing
MCP tool consolidation: method-dispatch pattern
description: Consolidate MCP tools using GitHub's method-dispatch pattern: one tool per resource with a method enum for verbs. skill(method: create|list|show|add|remove), personality(method: create|list|show|set), writing_style(method: create|list|show|set), ext(method: get|set|del|list). Also add slash command wrappers for all MCP tools. 13 attribute tools → 3, 4 ext tools → 1.

title: mission: TOCTOU between checkVerifierHash Load and renderVerifierBlock ReadFile
description: djb's Phase 3.5 frozen-evaluator review (ethos-07m.9) flagged a potential TOCTOU: Phase 3.3's checkVerifierHash does a lockless Store.Load of every matching open mission, and Phase 3.5's renderVerifierBlock then does a second lockless os.ReadFile of the same contract to get byte-for-byte bytes for the isolation block. A concurrent Store.Update between the two reads would inject contract bytes that differ from what the hash gate saw. Phase 3.5's threat model excludes adversarial concurrent mutations (the operator editing their own contract mid-spawn is the leader, not an attacker), and the pattern is inherited from Phase 3.3's existing lockless Load discipline. Not a blocker for 3.5 per djb's verdict. Fix options: (a) thread the contract bytes through checkVerifierHash so the first read's bytes are reused, or (b) add per-mission flocks around the read chain. File for Phase 3.6+ consideration if concurrent-update resistance becomes a threat-model concern.

title: Store.AppendResult/AppendReflection rollback writes empty file instead of removing
description: When AppendResult (or AppendReflection) rolls back the sibling file after an event-log append failure on a brand-new mission, the rollback calls writeResultsLocked (or writeReflectionsLocked) with an empty slice, producing results: null on disk instead of os.Remove-ing the file. Close gate and append-only invariants are still correct, but a stat check for file existence now sees an empty file where the original state was no file. Class fix across both AppendResult and AppendReflection. Add a small helper (removeResultsLocked / removeReflectionsLocked) that os.Remove-s and ignores os.IsNotExist. Add a sabotage test asserting file absence after rollback. Phase 3.4 reflection store has the identical pattern; fix both in one PR. Found by silent-failure-hunter during Phase 3.6 round 1 review. See feedback from djb, mdm, and feature-dev:code-reviewer on commit 83bb83a.

title: Go Report Card: reach 100% score
description: Current score: 97.95% (A+). Three issues: (1) gofmt -s on identity_test.go, (2) main() cyclomatic complexity 22 > 15 — extract command dispatch to map or helper, (3) runShow() cyclomatic complexity 16 > 15 — extract field printing loop.

title: --json flag advertised in help but not implemented
Installer should print plugin version being installed
description: install.sh builds the binary (prints version) but does not print which plugin version the marketplace resolves to. After 'claude plugin install ethos@punt-labs', the user cannot tell whether they got v0.2.0 or v0.3.0 without manually inspecting the cache. Add version output after plugin install step.
close_reason: PR #24 merged — installer now force-updates marketplace and verifies plugin version

title: feat: rich identity list output with columnar formatting
description:
Ethos identity list output is a flat comma-separated string. Biff /who shows aligned columns with headers (handle, tty, repo, idle, status, host). Ethos should match this quality.

## Current behavior
`identity list` returns: `bwk (Brian K), * claude (Claude Agento), ...`
Two-channel: panel shows the same flat string, additionalContext has JSON array.

## Target behavior

### MCP response (handleListIdentities)
Add personality and writing_style fields to the list entry struct. Currently only returns handle, name, kind, active.

### Two-channel display (formatIdentityList in format_output.go)
- Panel (updatedMCPToolOutput): `5 identities, 2 active`
- Context (additionalContext): aligned columnar table like biff:

```
HANDLE     NAME              KIND   PERSONALITY         ACTIVE
bwk        Brian K           agent  kernighan           -
claude     Claude Agento     agent  friendly-direct     *
jfreeman   Jim Freeman       human  principal-engineer  *
mdm        Doug M            agent  mcilroy             -
```

### Files to modify
1. `internal/mcp/tools.go` — handleListIdentities: add personality, writing_style to entry struct
2. `internal/hook/format_output.go` — formatIdentityList: columnar table with alignment
3. `internal/hook/format_output_test.go` — update test for new format

### Acceptance criteria
- Panel shows count summary
- Context shows aligned columnar table with headers
- Columns: HANDLE, NAME, KIND, PERSONALITY, ACTIVE
- Active column shows * for active, - for inactive
- Column widths adapt to content (use max width per column)
- `make check` passes

title: refactor: move iam from identity MCP tool to session
description: iam calls session.Store.Join() — belongs on session tool, not identity. Move MCP handler, update CLI (session iam canonical, ethos iam hidden), update slash commands and hook formatter. Per build-plan-projection.md Bead 3.

title: mission: walked-diff round delta for verifier isolation block (SC5)
description: Phase 3.5 (ethos-07m.9) deliberately deferred success criterion 5 — walked-diff round delta enumeration — to a follow-up. The isolation block currently lists the static write_set entries, not a real filesystem walk of files actually touched by the worker in the current round. A walked diff would use filepath.WalkDir over the write_set patterns to list the concrete files, giving the verifier a more precise picture of what to review. Adds work on every verifier spawn for marginal value over the write_set pattern list. File for Phase 3.6+ if operator feedback suggests the prose directive is too vague. See DES-035 for the full rationale.

title: feat: rich ext get/list output with key-value table
description: Ext get/list shows 'Extensions' in panel and raw JSON in context. Should show count summary in panel (N namespaces or N keys) and columnar key-value table in context (NAMESPACE, KEY, VALUE for get; NAMESPACE column for list). Use FormatTable.

title: Integration design: ethos + beadle, ethos + biff, ethos + quarry
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and three consumer systems. Critical constraint: ethos must NOT know about beadle, biff, or quarry — dependency flows one way. Consumers use ext/ to store their own data on identities.

Three integrations:

1. ethos + beadle: Agents get email addresses and credentials via ext/beadle. Beadle reads identity (name, handle, email field) from ethos sidecar. Beadle stores GPG keys, IMAP config in ext/beadle. Result: agents can send/receive email as themselves.

2. ethos + biff: Agents get team messaging via ext/biff. Biff reads identity for display name and handle. Biff stores preferred TTY, routing preferences in ext/biff. Result: agents can /write, /read, /talk, /plan, /finger as themselves. Session roster already bridges ethos and biff — needs audit for completeness.

3. ethos + quarry: Agents get persistent memory across sessions. Quarry stores per-agent collection or namespace. Past conversations, decisions, work artifacts searchable as the agent's own memory. This is the least proven integration — quarry currently indexes project files, not agent memories. May need a new quarry feature (agent-scoped collections) or may work with existing registration + namespace conventions.

All three integrations must follow the sidecar contract: consumer reads ethos state from filesystem or MCP tools. Consumer stores its own data in ext/<namespace>. Ethos never imports or calls consumer code. Review punt-kit/standards/ for existing integration patterns and confirm or revise.

Deliverable: design doc per integration, implementation beads, ext schema definitions. No code changes to ethos core — only ext data and consumer-side code.
close_reason: Split into 3 separate beads: ethos-g2f (beadle), ethos-wb4 (biff), ethos-c3v (quarry)

title: Update docs/workflow.md with full team roster and session lessons
description: docs/workflow.md needs updating: add all 10 team members (was only 3), persona animation section, teams/roles section, updated delegation table, epic dependency example, and lessons learned from 2026-03-25/26 session. Also add Team and Workflow reference in CLAUDE.md. Content drafted — apply after feat/repo-config-and-team-resolution merges.

title: fix: projection quick fixes (typo, --reference, writing column, roster alias)
description: Fix talent tool 'Skill slug' typo, add --reference to whoami, add WRITING column to CLI list, add session roster alias. All independent small fixes per build-plan-projection.md Bead 1.

title: fix: CLI ethos show output should match MCP two-channel quality
description: CLI 'ethos show <handle>' dumps raw field-by-field output with full markdown content inline. Should use FormatTable for the identity summary fields (FIELD, VALUE columns) and show attribute content below in a structured way. Match the quality bar set by the identity list columnar output.

title: Consolidate duplicate Identity struct — cmd/ethos/identity.go vs internal/identity/
description: cmd/ethos/identity.go has a parallel Identity struct with YAML I/O baked in. internal/identity/ has the canonical model. Copilot review flagged this duplication. Consolidate into internal/identity/ with proper separation of model and persistence.

title: show command omits writing_style, personality, and skills fields
investigate Agent isolation:worktree behavior with ethos sub-agent types
description: During Phase 3.2 (ethos-07m.6, mission m-2026-04-08-001), the COO delegated bwk with Agent isolation:worktree and run_in_background:true. The agent's changes landed in main's working tree, not in .claude/worktrees/agent-XXXX/. git worktree list showed only main. Hypothesis (unverified): the worktree wrapper sets the agent's CWD but does not intercept absolute paths in tool calls. The COO's prompt used absolute paths throughout. Alternative: ethos sub-agent types may not honor isolation:worktree at all. This is a workflow primitive failure that compounded into COE-9 (concurrent file modification corrupted leader's reads) and is a latent risk for any future round where leader and worker share the same working tree. Run a controlled experiment: spawn a no-op ethos sub-agent with isolation:worktree, observe whether git worktree list shows the worktree, observe whether absolute-path Write calls land in main or the worktree. Document the finding as a memory entry and update the COE. Full COE entry at .tmp/coe/2026-04-08-phase-3.2-workflow-violations.md (COE-4).

title: refactor(cli): migrate to cobra per cli.md Go standard
description: punt-kit/standards/cli.md now specifies cobra as the Go CLI framework (PR #139). ethos uses hand-rolled arg parsing with the same bugs beadle had: no --flag=value support, no per-flag help, global flags not propagating to subcommands. Migrate all subcommands to cobra following DES-014 pattern from beadle.

title: PreCompact hook uses invalid hookSpecificOutput schema — should use systemMessage
Generate .claude/agents/*.md from ethos identity data
description:
Generate .claude/agents/<handle>.md from ethos identity data, aligned with Claude Code's official sub-agent architecture. Full specification: ethos/docs/agent-identity-spec.tex (compiled PDF: agent-identity-spec.pdf).

## Summary

Three mechanisms deliver identity to sub-agents:
1. Agent definition file (static, generated by SessionStart) — frontmatter + personality body
2. SubagentStart hook (dynamic, runtime) — persona block + extensions + parent context
3. CLAUDE.md (inherited, loaded separately) — org standards

## Decomposed into Sub-Beads

- ethos-nri (P1 bug): SubagentStart hook does not inject extension session_context
- ethos-l9d (P1 task): Create baseline-ops skill for operational discipline
- ethos-5po (this bead): Agent definition generation in SessionStart

## Implementation Phases

Phase 1: baseline-ops skill (ethos-l9d)
Phase 2: SubagentStart extension fix (ethos-nri)
Phase 3: Agent definition generation (this bead)
Phase 4: Role-based hook generation
Phase 5: Rollout — replace hand-written agents across all repos

## Acceptance Criteria

See agent-identity-spec.tex Sections 6-7 for full criteria.
close_reason: PR #146 merged. SessionStart generates .claude/agents/*.md from identity data.

title: Add architecture.tex documentation
close_reason: PR #36 merged — architecture.tex and doc improvements

description: Attribute list outputs (talent list, personality list, writing-style list) show flat comma-separated slugs. Should show count summary in panel (N talents) and columnar table in context using FormatTable (SLUG column). Consistent with identity list standard.

feat: move voice field to ext/vox
description: Remove voice from Identity struct, migrate to ext/vox extension. Schema change prerequisite for repo-scoped config.

title: refactor: hook standards compliance
Agent team teammates have no ethos session
description:
Agent team teammates have no ethos session.

PROVEN ROOT CAUSE: FindClaudePID() fails for teammates because the binary name is a version number, not 'claude'.

Evidence chain:
1. Hooks DO fire for teammates — quarry SessionStart, beads bd prime, and ethos SessionStart all fire. MCP servers and skills all load. (Tested with diagnostic teammates.)
2. The ethos hook receives a valid session_id in stdin payload: {"session_id":"92438591-...","hook_event_name":"SessionStart",...}
3. But FindClaudePID() in internal/process/tree.go calls isClaudeComm() which checks filepath.Base(comm) == "claude".
4. The lead's claude process was launched via symlink ~/.local/bin/claude → .local/share/claude/versions/2.1.86. ps -o comm shows 'claude'. isClaudeComm returns true. Session created.
5. The teammate's claude process was spawned directly by the lead's binary. ps -o comm shows '/Users/jfreeman/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.86'. filepath.Base returns '2.1.86'. isClaudeComm('2.1.86') returns false.
6. FindClaudePID falls back to os.Getppid() — a transient PID that dies. Session PID file becomes orphaned. ethos session roster returns 'No active session.'

FIX: isClaudeComm (or the full path check in readProc) must also match version-named binaries under the claude versions directory. Biff solved the same problem in DES-011a (find_session_key in src/biff/session_key.py).

Specific code: internal/process/tree.go:64-70 — isClaudeComm needs to match paths containing '/claude/versions/' or version strings like '2.1.86' when the parent path is the claude versions directory.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #126. FindClaudePID now recognizes version-named Claude Code binaries on macOS and Linux.

title: Extension mechanism — persona-level ext store and CLI
description: Implement DES-008: persona.ext/ directory with namespaced YAML files, merged view in Store.Load, ethos ext get/set/del/list commands, ext_get/ext_set/ext_del/ext_list MCP tools, validation constraints.

title: mission: add corrupt-existing-mission test for fatal Load failure invariant
description: djb's Phase 3.2 review (ethos-07m.6) suggested a targeted test that would strengthen the 'Load failure on existing mission is fatal' guarantee in checkWriteSetConflicts. The current code path returns a wrapped error on any Load failure (silently skipping a corrupt mission would defeat the conflict check). Existing tests around Store.Load cover decode errors generically, but no test makes the security invariant explicit at the conflict-check boundary. Proposed test: in store_test.go, write a deliberately corrupted YAML file under missions/, then call Store.Create on a new mission and assert the error wraps the Load failure rather than admitting the new mission. djb noted: 'Not a 3.2 gap — the spec covers the fatal-error path and existing tests around Store.Load cover decode errors — but a targeted test would make the security invariant explicit in the test suite.'

title: Remove Python-hardcoded quality gate hints from prod quarry
description: hint_rules.py lines 101-107 hardcode Python quality gate tools (ruff, mypy, pyright, pytest) in the sequence rule that fires on every git commit. This fires in all projects including Go, reminding users to run Python tools that don't exist. Fix: replace hardcoded Python gates with language-agnostic make check, per makefile.md standard. Dev-specific conventions should not ship in prod plugin code.
close_reason: Created in wrong rig, recreating in quarry

title: Phase 2: Production-Quality Agents
description: Make generated agent definitions as effective as hand-crafted ones. Anti-responsibilities, role-based hooks, structured output, baseline-ops injection, mission skill. See docs/ETHOS-ROADMAP.md Phase 2.

title: Generate anti-responsibilities from team collaboration graph
description: GenerateAgentFiles reads collaboration graph and emits 'What You Don't Do' section. If implementer reports_to coo, generate 'Don't make architectural decisions — the COO handles that.' See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §2.1.

title: Generate role-based hooks in agent frontmatter
description: Roles with write tools get PostToolUse hook (make check after Write|Edit). Review-only roles get no hooks. Hook templates per role category in GenerateAgentFiles. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §2.2.

title: Add output_format field to Role for structured handoff
description: Optional markdown template on Role. GenerateAgentFiles includes as 'Output Format' section. Machine-readable fields: files changed, verdict, confidence, open questions. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §2.3.

title: Inject baseline-ops skill in generated agent frontmatter
description: GenerateAgentFiles always includes skills: [baseline-ops] in frontmatter. Depends on ethos-l9d (baseline-ops skill exists). See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §2.4.

title: Build /mission skill (MVP — skill file)
description: Ship ~/.claude/skills/mission/SKILL.md. Structured delegation skill: resolve agent from team, scaffold mission contract (inputs/outputs/success criteria/files_owned/constraints), confirm, spawn. Phase A of mission-skill-design.md. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §2.6.

title: GenerateAgentFiles swallows LoadRepoConfig errors
description: generate_agents.go:24-26 returns nil for ALL LoadRepoConfig errors, not just file-not-found. Config parse errors and permission errors are silently ignored. Should distinguish ErrNotFound from other errors. Pre-existing, found during Phase 1 local review.

title: GenerateAgentFiles reports success on partial failure
description: generate_agents.go:99-101 only returns error when ALL expected agents fail (generated==0). If 5/10 generate, returns nil. Should report partial failures. Pre-existing, found during Phase 1 local review.

title: reflection: containsControlChar on Reason field
description: Reflection.Reason is not checked for control characters in reflection.go Validate. Author and signals are checked; Reason is not. Low severity: requires write access to the operator's own reflections file, but rule-5 consistency argues for the check. Fix: add containsControlChar(r.Reason) check in reflection.go Validate around line 144. djb's frozen-evaluator review of Phase 3.4 (ethos-07m.8) flagged this as 'worth a future bead but not blocking' during the PASS 0.96 verdict.

title: CLI exits 1 instead of 2 for flag/arg errors
description: The punt-kit CLI standard specifies exit 2 for usage errors (missing positional, bad flag). Cobra defaults to exit 1 with an 'Error:' prefix. Every ethos mission subcommand inherits this. Fix via cmd.SilenceErrors + cmd.SilenceUsage + a root handler that maps cobra errors to exit 2. Deferred from Phase 3.7 round 2 per mdm — pre-existing across every mission subcommand, not introduced by that PR.

title: feat: rich session roster output with columnar formatting
description: Session roster shows 'Roster loaded' in panel and raw JSON in context. Should show count summary in panel (N participants) and columnar table in context (AGENT_ID, PERSONA, PARENT, TYPE, matching biff /who style). Use FormatTable with ▶ prefix.

title: Integration design: ethos + quarry — agents with persistent memory
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and quarry. Dependency flows one way: quarry reads ethos, ethos never knows about quarry. This is the least proven integration — needs design exploration before implementation.

What it enables: agents have persistent memory across sessions. Past conversations, decisions, work artifacts, and reasoning are searchable and recallable as the agent's own experience. When Claude starts a new session, it can recall 'I worked on persona animation last week' from quarry.

Integration points:
- Quarry reads identity (handle) from ethos to scope memory to the agent
- Quarry stores collection name, search preferences, retention policy in ext/quarry on the identity
- Memory ingestion: session transcripts, bead history, commit messages, PR summaries attributed to the agent
- Memory retrieval: semantic search scoped to the agent's handle, surfaced at session start or on demand

Open questions:
- Per-agent collection vs. agent-scoped namespace within project collections?
- What gets ingested? Full session transcripts are large. Summaries? Key decisions? Bead close messages?
- How does memory survive across repos? Agent memory is personal, not project-scoped.
- Is quarry the right mechanism? It indexes files on disk today. Agent memory may need a different model (event stream vs. document corpus).
- How does the auto-memory system in Claude Code (.claude/projects/*/memory/) relate? Complement or replace?

Ext schema (ext/quarry):
- collection: quarry collection name for this agent's memory
- retention_days: how long to keep memory (0 = forever)
- auto_ingest: what to auto-capture (sessions, beads, commits, none)

Deliverable: design exploration doc, decision on mechanism, ext schema definition, implementation beads if quarry is confirmed as the right approach. No changes to ethos core.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete. PRs #126 (PID fix) and #127 (memory hooks) merged. Agents with ext/quarry configured get memory instructions in SessionStart and PreCompact. Quarry side done in PR #147 + #150.

title: PostToolUse hook error on whoami MCP call
description: Root cause: same as ethos-dc7 — installed plugin is v0.2.0 with old suppress-output.sh. The PostToolUse hook runs the v0.2.0 version which may be failing on the v0.3.0 MCP tool response format. Fix: same as ethos-dc7, update marketplace.
close_reason: Marketplace PR #79 merged, plugin now installs v0.3.0

title: punt release: call release-plugin.sh for is_plugin projects, not just is_hybrid
description: punt release only calls scripts/release-plugin.sh for is_hybrid projects (Python + plugin). Pure plugin projects like ethos (Go + plugin) also have release scripts but they are never called. This caused v0.4.0 to ship with ethos-dev name and -dev command files. Fix: release.py lines 280 and 750 should check is_plugin too.

title: Hire the team: 6 new agent identities + shared team repo
description: Create 6 new agent identities (djb, adt, ghr, edt, ach, adb) with personalities, writing styles, talents, and Claude Code agent definitions. Create punt-labs/team repo as the single source of truth for all identities. Use git submodule at .punt-labs/ethos/ in each project repo to share the team across 25+ repos. Integrate with punt init for automatic setup. Design doc: docs/persona-animation.md (layer 4: expression). Inspired-by figures: Bernstein (security), Turing (formal methods PM), Hopper (dev tools PM), Tufte (UX), Hamilton (finance/ops), Lovelace (infra).
close_reason: Phases 1-5 complete. Phase 6 (punt init) deferred to separate punt-kit PR.

title: Phase 1: Role as first-class ethos concept
description: Add Role as a first-class concept in ethos. Roles are global (reusable across teams). Storage: .punt-labs/ethos/roles/<name>.yaml with global fallback. Schema: name, responsibilities (list), permissions (list). New internal/role/ package with Store (CRUD, YAML persistence). CLI: ethos role create/list/show/delete. MCP: role tool with method dispatch. Per Z spec (docs/teams.tex): roles keyed by name, global across teams. Delegate to bwk.

title: Phase 2: Team as first-class ethos concept
description: Add Team as a first-class concept. Teams bind identities to roles for a set of repositories. Storage: .punt-labs/ethos/teams/<name>.yaml. Schema: name, repositories (list), members (identity+role bindings), collaborations (from_role, to_role, type). New internal/team/ package with Store, Member, Collaboration structs. Validation enforces Z spec invariants: referential integrity (members reference valid identities and roles), non-empty teams, collaboration validity (both roles filled by members), no self-collaboration. CLI: ethos team create/list/show/add-member/remove-member/add-collab/delete. MCP: team tool with method dispatch. Delegate to bwk.

title: Phase 3: Create punt-labs/team shared identity repo
description: Create the punt-labs/team GitHub repo as the single source of truth for all identities, personalities, writing styles, talents, roles, teams, and agent definitions. Move existing 4 identities (jfreeman, claude, bwk, mdm) from ethos .punt-labs/ethos/. Create 6 new agent identities with full personalities, writing styles, and talents: djb (security, Bernstein), adt (PM grounding, Turing), ghr (PM building blocks, Hopper), edt (UX/visual, Tufte), ach (finance/ops, Hamilton), adb (infra/platform, Lovelace). Create 10 role definitions. Create team definitions (engineering, website). Create .claude/agents/*.md for all 8 agent identities. Structure per build-plan-teams.md.

title: Phase 4: Wire team repo as git submodule
description: Replace .punt-labs/ethos/ contents in ethos repo with git submodule pointing to punt-labs/team. LayeredStore already reads from .punt-labs/ethos/ so no Go code changes needed. Verify: ethos show claude, ethos role list, ethos team show engineering all resolve from submodule. Pilot: add same submodule to biff repo and verify identity resolution works cross-repo.

title: Phase 5: SessionStart agent definition installer
description: Extend SessionStart hook to install agent definitions from the submodule into .claude/agents/. After persona injection, scan .punt-labs/ethos/agents/*.md and copy any missing or changed files to .claude/agents/. Same pattern as vox/biff command deployment. Idempotent: only copy if file doesn't exist or content differs. Log deployed agents to stderr. Do not overwrite user-modified definitions (compare content, not mtime). Delegate to bwk.

title: Phase 6: punt init auto-adds team submodule
description: Update punt init to: (1) add the punt-labs/team git submodule at .punt-labs/ethos/ for punt-labs org repos, (2) scaffold .punt-labs/ethos.yaml with sensible defaults (agent: claude, team: engineering). Update punt audit to check that the submodule exists and is up to date, and that ethos.yaml is present. Separate punt-kit PR. Changes in src/punt_kit/init.py and src/punt_kit/audit.py.

title: feat: implement LayeredStore (repo-local → user-global)
description: LayeredStore wraps repo + global Store instances. Load checks repo first, fallback to global. Ext always global. Includes layered attribute resolution and ValidateRefs across both roots.

title: SessionStart hook error: installed plugin is v0.2.0, binary is v0.3.0
description: Root cause: punt release built and tagged v0.3.0 but did not update the marketplace. The installed plugin at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/punt-labs/ethos/0.2.0/ has old hooks without MCP auto-allow, dev mode detection, or new commands. The binary at ~/.local/bin/ethos is v0.3.0 with new session subcommands. Version mismatch causes session-start.sh to call session subcommands that exist in v0.3.0 binary but the hook itself is v0.2.0 code. Fix: update marketplace.json ref to v0.3.0 tag, then claude plugin update.
close_reason: Marketplace PR #79 merged, plugin now installs v0.3.0

title: fix: stale PID files in sessions/current not cleaned up
docs: DES-018 repo-scoped identity, DES-019 voice as extension
description: Add design decisions, update storage layout tables in CLAUDE.md, README.md, AGENTS.md, DESIGN.md.

title: Integration design: ethos + beadle — agents with email
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and beadle. Dependency flows one way: beadle reads ethos, ethos never knows about beadle.

What it enables: agents send and receive email as themselves. Claude Agento sends from claude@punt-labs.com with GPG signing. Each agent identity has its own email address, credentials, and contact permissions.

Integration points:
- Beadle reads identity (name, handle, email field) from ethos sidecar at known filesystem paths
- Beadle stores GPG key ID, IMAP config, trust preferences in ext/beadle on the identity
- Beadle resolves identity per-request — no cached state, always reads current ethos identity
- Multi-identity: different agents in the same session can send as different addresses

Ext schema (ext/beadle):
- gpg_key_id: GPG key fingerprint for signing
- imap_host, imap_port: mail server config (if not using Proton Bridge defaults)
- trust_default: default trust level for unknown senders

Existing state: beadle already has partial ethos integration (reads email field, uses ext/beadle for GPG). Needs audit for completeness — are all integration points using the sidecar contract correctly? Does multi-identity work end-to-end?

Deliverable: design doc, ext schema definition, audit of current integration, implementation beads for gaps. No changes to ethos core.

title: PreCompact output: fix formatting, add team member talents/writing style, deduplicate
Phase 4: Operational Excellence
description: Hook enhancements, security, observability. Context loading, safety constraints, write-set admission, audit logging, audio notifications. See docs/ETHOS-ROADMAP.md Phase 4.

title: SessionStart: inject working context (git branch, uncommitted changes)
description: Extend HandleSessionStart to emit git branch, uncommitted changes alongside persona context. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §4.1.

title: Role-based safety constraints for pre-tool hooks
description: Safety constraint field on Role consumed by pre-tool hooks. Reviewer should never write files; researcher should never execute destructive commands. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §4.2.

title: Session audit logging in PostToolUse hook
description: Append-only audit log of tool invocations: timestamp, session ID, agent, tool, decision, reason. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §4.4.

title: PreCompact hook emits full persona + team context
firstContentLine truncates multi-line sentences in persona opening
description: BuildPersonaBlock uses firstContentLine to extract the opening descriptor from personality markdown. When the first content line wraps across multiple lines (e.g., 'Principles from the Unix philosophy and\nMcIlroy'\''s work on software componentization.'), only the first line is extracted, producing a truncated sentence: 'Principles from the Unix philosophy and.' Fix: extract until the first sentence boundary (period followed by whitespace/newline/EOF) or until a blank line.

title: Symlink policy across mission loaders (defense in depth)
description: LoadEvents, LoadReflections, LoadResults, and Store.Load all follow symlinks via os.ReadFile without checking whether the target is inside missionsDir. An attacker with write access to ~/.punt-labs/ethos/missions/ can plant a symlink that redirects a reader at arbitrary host content. Mitigation: add an os.Lstat check before os.ReadFile that refuses symlinks whose resolved target is outside missionsDir. Apply to all four loaders in a single change. Deferred from Phase 3.7 round 2 (m-2026-04-08-006) per djb's recommendation — fixing one loader but not the others would create asymmetry worse than the current consistent weakness.

title: Phase 5: Ecosystem
description: Long-term investments: starter team templates, agent marketplace, cross-tool workflow orchestration. See docs/ETHOS-ROADMAP.md Phase 5.

title: Create baseline-ops skill for sub-agent operational discipline
description:
Sub-agents that load via subagent_type get their agent definition as the ENTIRE system prompt, replacing Claude Code's default. They lose all generic operational guidance (tool usage, verification, output formatting).

The official mechanism to inject content at sub-agent startup is the skills frontmatter field. Create a baseline-ops skill that every generated agent preloads.

## Location

~/.claude/skills/baseline-ops/SKILL.md (user-level, available everywhere)

## Content (~50-80 lines)

1. Tool usage: Read over cat, Grep over grep, Glob over find
2. Verification: make check after every change, zero violations
3. No commits: return results to COO
4. Conventions: look at neighbors, follow existing patterns
5. Scope: no features beyond spec, no unnecessary comments
6. Security: no OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities, no logged secrets
7. Progress: use TodoWrite for multi-step work
8. Output: concise, no preamble

## Frontmatter

---
name: baseline-ops
description: Operational discipline for Punt Labs sub-agents
user-invocable: false
disable-model-invocation: true
---

## Verification

Spawn a sub-agent with skills: [baseline-ops] and ask how to read a file. It should say Read tool, not cat.

## Reference

See agent-identity-spec.tex Section 4.

title: docs: surface projection documentation (CLAUDE.md, DES-021, README, AGENTS)
description: Update CLAUDE.md with surface projection section. Add DES-021 (session create/delete CLI-only deferred decision). Update README and AGENTS tool references. Per build-plan-projection.md Bead 5.

title: Test coverage at 2.2% — cmd/ethos, resolve, and mcp packages have 0%
Rich identity attributes: markdown references for skills, personality, writing style
description: Convert identity attributes from inline strings to markdown file references. Each attribute type gets its own directory under ~/.punt-labs/ethos/. An identity becomes a unique combination of reusable attribute files plus core identity fields (name, handle, kind, email, github, voice, agent). No backward compatibility needed — no external users. Directories: skills/, personalities/, writing-styles/. Each .md file is a self-contained definition. The identity YAML references paths. This enables reuse: two agents can share a personality but have different skills.

title: Skills as markdown files in ~/.punt-labs/ethos/skills/
description: Create skills/ directory. Each skill is a .md file defining what the identity can do, domain expertise, tools, approach. Identity YAML skills field changes from string list to path list. Examples: formal-methods.md, product-strategy.md, software-engineering.md. Same format for humans and agents — a human's skill file describes their expertise and standards, an agent's describes its capabilities and tools.

title: Personality as markdown file in ~/.punt-labs/ethos/personalities/
description: Create personalities/ directory. Each personality is a .md file defining behavioral patterns, communication tendencies, decision-making approach, values. Identity YAML personality field changes from inline string to a path. Examples: principal-engineer.md, friendly-mentor.md. Reusable — multiple identities can share a personality.

title: Writing style as markdown file in ~/.punt-labs/ethos/writing-styles/
description: Create writing-styles/ directory. Each writing style is a .md file defining written communication patterns — sentence structure, vocabulary, formatting preferences, anti-patterns, examples. Separate from personality (how you think/act) and from voice (how you sound). Identity YAML writing_style field changes from inline string to a path. Examples: concise-quantified.md, academic-formal.md.

title: Update identity schema and store for markdown attribute references
description: Update Identity struct: skills becomes []string of paths, personality becomes string path, writing_style becomes string path. Update Store to validate that referenced files exist on load. Update MCP tools (get_identity, create_identity, whoami) to resolve paths and include file contents in responses. Update ethos show to render resolved attributes. Remove inline string support — clean break.

title: Migrate existing identities (jfreeman, claude) to markdown attributes
description: Create the actual .md files for both identities. jfreeman: skills (executive.md, software-engineering.md, product-management.md), personality, writing style. claude: skills (management.md, delegation.md, product-development.md, engineering.md), personality, writing style. Update both identity YAML files to reference paths instead of inline strings.

title: Installer deploys sidecar README files to ~/.punt-labs/ethos/
description: The repo sidecar/ directory contains README.md files for each subdirectory of ~/.punt-labs/ethos/. install.sh must copy these during installation so users and consuming tools have documentation of the file layout and sidecar contract. Use rsync or cp -r with --no-clobber to avoid overwriting user data.

title: SubagentStart hook does not inject extension session_context
description:
HandleSubagentStart (internal/hook/subagent_start.go) builds a persona block with personality, writing style, and talents but does NOT read extension session_context blocks from <handle>.ext/<tool>.yaml.

This means sub-agents do not receive:
- Quarry memory instructions (collection, search/remember commands, memory types)
- Vox voice configuration
- Beadle GPG key config
- Any future tool-scoped session context

The main session receives these via SessionStart (which calls BuildSessionContext including extensions). Sub-agents get only the persona block from SubagentStart.

## Fix

In HandleSubagentStart, after building the persona block:
1. Read all files in <handle>.ext/*.yaml
2. For each file containing a session_context key, append the value to additionalContext
3. If no extensions exist, skip silently

## Tests needed

- Identity with extensions → additionalContext includes session_context
- Identity without extensions → works as before
- Malformed extension file → skipped with warning, other extensions still processed

## Reference

See agent-identity-spec.tex Section 3.2.2 (Gap: Extension Session Context).
close_reason: PR #152 merged. SubagentStart injects extension session_context. Also fixed globalStore→identityStore for repo-local resolution.

title: session list/show: align display format with biff
description: Alignment changes with no schema modifications. (1) session list: display started as 'Sun Mar 29 14:22' instead of ISO. (2) session list: show short session IDs (first 8 chars). (3) Rename roster to show as the canonical verb. (4) Consistent table formatting with biff (arrow prefix, column alignment). (5) session iam: require --session when outside Claude Code PID tree.
close_reason: Session display aligned with biff format. PR #141 merged.

title: feat-dev: helper for cobra MarkFlagRequired pattern
description: mdm flagged in PR #176 review (cycle 2): _ = cmd.MarkFlagRequired("file") silently swallows the error if the flag name is mistyped, leaving the flag unmarked. The error only fires when the flag doesn't exist (a programming error that should panic). Cross-file tech debt in cmd/ethos/ — every ethos command uses this pattern. Fix: add a helper like mustMarkRequired(cmd, name) that panics on programming errors so a typo is caught at startup instead of producing a silent regression. Apply consistently across cmd/ethos/session.go, mission.go, identity.go, etc. Spotted while building Phase 3.1 mission contract primitive.

title: DES-012: namespace all commands under /ethos:*
description: Remove top-level command deployment from session-start.sh, add stale file cleanup, create missing identity command files, update docs. Per DES-012 in DESIGN.md.

title: ethos identity list: ACTIVE column always shows dash
description: ethos identity list shows ACTIVE: - for every identity even when biff who shows active sessions. The active column is not reading biff session data to determine which identities have live sessions.
close_reason: ACTIVE column removed from CLI, MCP, and hook output. PR #140 merged.

title: fix: add PostToolUse formatters for team and role MCP tools
description: Team and role MCP tools return raw JSON — no PostToolUse formatter exists. The format_output.go switch statement handles identity, talent, personality, writing_style, session, ext, doctor but not team or role. These fall through to the default truncate(result, 200) which shows a useless JSON blob. Need to add formatTeam and formatRole functions matching the existing patterns (columnar tables via FormatTable, two-channel output via emit).

title: refactor: extract IdentityStore interface
description: Define Go interface for identity store operations. Prerequisite for LayeredStore. Change resolve.Resolve, mcp.Handler, CLI helpers to accept interface.

title: Persona animation: inject behavioral content at session lifecycle hooks
description: Ethos declares identity but never injects the behavioral content (personality, writing style) into the session context. Implement 3 phases: (1) SessionStart injects full persona block, (2) PreCompact re-injects for compaction survival, (3) SubagentStart injects subagent persona at spawn. Design doc: docs/persona-animation.md

title: Session roster — multi-participant identity awareness
description: Any participant (human or agent) in a session needs to know who they are and who everyone else is. Current single-slot active file cannot express multi-participant sessions. Requires a session-scoped roster with propagation to subagents. See DES-007 in DESIGN.md for open questions and constraints.
close_reason: v0.1.0 released — session roster, identity CRUD, extensions, MCP server all shipped

title: Rename ext persona parameter to handle for consistency
Marketplace not updated to v0.3.0: plugin install pulls old code
description: Root cause: punt release tagged v0.3.0 and created the GitHub release but did NOT update the marketplace (claude-plugins repo) to point to the v0.3.0 tag. When users run 'claude plugin install ethos@punt-labs', they get v0.2.0 code (old hooks, old commands, no MCP auto-allow). The install.sh correctly installs the v0.3.0 BINARY (via go install) but the PLUGIN comes from the marketplace which still points to the old ref. Fix: update marketplace.json in punt-labs/claude-plugins to point ethos at the v0.3.0 tag.
close_reason: Marketplace PR #79 merged, plugin now installs v0.3.0

title: Session roster — session store, lifecycle hooks, CLI
description: Implement DES-007: session roster YAML at sessions/<id>.yaml, flock-based concurrency, process tree walk for Claude PID, ethos session/iam commands, session MCP tools, SubagentStart/SubagentStop/SessionEnd hooks, PID-keyed session ID propagation, purge command.

title: Sub-agents denied Edit/Write despite bypassPermissions and tool allowlist
description: All sub-agents (bwk, general-purpose) are denied Edit/Write/Bash on files within the project. settings.json has Read/Edit/Write(../**) which uses relative ../ glob — may not resolve correctly for sub-agents. Affects all delegated code work. Root cause needs investigation: is it the ../ glob, the bypassPermissions propagation, or something else?

title: Fix PreCompact hook output schema — use systemMessage instead of hookSpecificOutput
feat: repo-scoped identity configuration
description: Move identity definitions (identities, talents, personalities, writing styles) from user-global (~/.punt-labs/ethos/) to repo-local (.punt-labs/ethos/) with user-global overrides for PII fields. Resolution chain: repo-local → user-global → error.

title: DES-022: Extension-provided session context
description: Replace BuildMemorySection (quarry-specific Go code) with generic BuildExtensionContext that emits session_context from any extension verbatim. Move quarry instructions from memory.go into ext/quarry.yaml session_context field. See DES-022 in DESIGN.md and docs/quarry-integration.md for full design. Migration: move instruction text to ext files, replace BuildMemorySection calls, delete memory.go.
close_reason: DES-022 implemented: BuildExtensionContext replaces BuildMemorySection, PR #135 merged

title: FormatLocalTime renders year and timezone offset
description: hook.FormatLocalTime currently renders 'Mon Jan _2 15:04' — no year, no zone. Mission log is a post-mortem tool; two operators in different timezones see different bullets for the same event. Change FormatLocalTime to render '2006-01-02 15:04 MST' or RFC3339 with offset. Touches every caller: mission show, mission list, mission log, session output, MCP formatters. Deferred from Phase 3.7 round 2 per mdm — changing only mission log would create subcommand-family drift worse than the post-mortem ambiguity.

title: write_set: reject BOM and zero-width Unicode in validateWriteSetEntry
description: djb's frozen-evaluator review of Phase 3.2 (ethos-07m.6) flagged a defense-in-depth gap: containsControlChar in internal/mission/validate.go only rejects C0 controls (<0x20) and DEL (0x7F), and strings.TrimSpace does not strip BOM (U+FEFF). A write_set entry like \ufeffinternal/foo would compare as distinct from internal/foo through the conflict check. Not exploitable on POSIX filesystems where these are genuinely distinct filenames, but a hardening item: extend validateWriteSetEntry to reject zero-width characters (BOM, ZWSP, ZWNJ, ZWJ) alongside control characters. Documented in DES-032 round 4 as a follow-up. djb verdict noted: 'a defense-in-depth hardening item, not a correctness bug in Phase 3.2's stated scope.'

title: Integration design: ethos + biff — agents with team messaging
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and biff. Dependency flows one way: biff reads ethos, ethos never knows about biff.

What it enables: agents appear in /who, send messages via /write, have real-time conversations via /talk, set plans via /plan, and are discoverable via /finger — all as their own identity.

Integration points:
- Biff reads identity (name, handle) from ethos sidecar for display name in /who and message attribution
- Biff stores preferred TTY, routing preferences, notification settings in ext/biff on the identity
- Session roster already bridges ethos and biff — biff reads participant list to know who is in the session
- /plan and /finger reflect the agent's current work and availability

Ext schema (ext/biff):
- tty: current TTY session name
- routing: message routing preferences (e.g., prefer /talk over /write)
- notifications: notification level (all, mentions, none)

Existing state: biff already reads the session roster and shows agents in /who. The ext/biff namespace exists. Needs audit: is /plan per-agent? Does /finger resolve identity correctly for agents? Does /talk work agent-to-agent?

Deliverable: design doc, ext schema definition, audit of current integration, implementation beads for gaps. No changes to ethos core.

title: refactor: consolidate CLI identity commands under identity group
description: Add ethos identity <method> subcommand group matching MCP structure. Hide old top-level shortcuts (list, show, create) except whoami. Per build-plan-projection.md Bead 2.

title: feat: wire LayeredStore into CLI and MCP
description: Replace DefaultStore() with layered factory. Update Save/Create to write repo-local by default. --global flag for user-global.

title: Plugin parity: end-to-end install + runtime fixes
mission: tool-level file allowlist enforcement for verifier isolation (SC6)
description: Phase 3.5 (ethos-07m.9) deliberately deferred success criterion 6 — mechanical file-allowlist enforcement — to a follow-up. The isolation block currently declares the allowlist as a prose directive. A misbehaving verifier subagent could still call Read, Grep, or Glob against a path outside the list. Phase 3.5's threat model was 'cooperating verifier, not adversarial'; prose is sufficient. For defense-in-depth against an adversarial verifier, add a PreToolUse hook handler that checks the tool call's target path against the allowlist derived from the mission's write_set + contract file. The allowlist needs to be communicated from SubagentStart (where the verifier spawn is detected) to PreToolUse (where the tool call happens), probably via an env var or a session-scoped scratch file. See DES-035 for the full rationale on why this was scope-deferred.

title: feat: repo-level identity config and team resolution
description: Two capabilities bundled: (1) Move repo config from .punt-labs/ethos/config.yaml to .punt-labs/ethos.yaml — decouples config from the team submodule path. Supports agent: field (existing) and team: field (new). Works whether .punt-labs/ethos/ is a submodule or local data. (2) Team-by-repo lookup — given a repo, resolve which team works on it, who the members are, their roles, collaborations, and talents. Requires FindByRepo on team store, CLI command (ethos team for-repo), and MCP method. Together these let a repo declare its agent and team binding, and let tools query the full identity context for any repo.

title: feat: rich identity whoami/get context output
description: Identity whoami and get show a multi-line field list in panel (good) but raw JSON in context. Context should be the same formatted field list as the panel, or a richer version with resolved attribute content summaries. Raw JSON is not useful as context for the model.

title: session schema: add repo, host, joined fields
description: Extend Roster and Participant schemas. (1) Add repo (org/name from git remote) to Roster, written at session creation by SessionStart hook. (2) Add host (short hostname) to Roster, written at session creation. (3) Add joined (ISO timestamp) to Participant, written when participant joins via iam/join. (4) session list gains REPO column. (5) session show gains JOINED column. (6) Duration computed at display time from timestamps.
close_reason: Session schema extended with repo, host, joined. PR #143 merged.

title: feat: session list CLI + doctor MCP tool
description: Add ethos session list (summary per session: ID, count, primary persona). Add doctor MCP tool returning formatted check results (DES-020). Per build-plan-projection.md Bead 4.

title: Phase 1: Batteries Included — ship pre-built content
description: Ship content that makes ethos useful out of the box: baseline-ops skill, 6 starter roles, 10 starter talents, model field on Role. See docs/ETHOS-ROADMAP.md Phase 1.

title: Ship 6 starter roles (implementer, reviewer, researcher, architect, security-reviewer, test-engineer)
description: YAML files in sidecar/roles/, deployed by installer. Each role defines tools, responsibilities, and anti-responsibility patterns. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §1.2.

title: Ship 10 starter talents (code-review, testing, go, python, typescript, security, cli-design, api-design, documentation, devops)
description: Substantial markdown files (200-500 lines each) in sidecar/talents/, deployed by installer. Real domain expertise, not thin summaries. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §1.3.

title: Add model field to Role struct
description: Add Model string field to Role struct in internal/role/. GenerateAgentFiles includes model in frontmatter when non-empty. Default: inherit. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §1.4.

title: Installer deploys sidecar starter content to global ethos directories
description: install.sh seeds ~/.punt-labs/ethos/roles/ and ~/.punt-labs/ethos/talents/ with starter content from sidecar/. Also deploys baseline-ops skill to ~/.claude/skills/. Uses cp -n (no clobber) to preserve user customizations. Repo-local content in .punt-labs/ethos/ overrides global via layered store.
